title: 2011 Press Releases

source.name: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

summary: This source is an archive index page listing all Federal Reserve press releases from calendar year 2011. It is a navigation/listing page containing hundreds of distinct documents including FOMC statements, enforcement actions, banking applications, regulatory proposals, and other announcements. As a listing page, it does not constitute a single archiveable research document.
key_points: This is a listing page (listing_page), not a single document - per rule 6, must be treated as non-archiveable
Contains press releases spanning the entire 2011 calendar year (December 2011 back to at least September 2011 visible)
Includes multiple categories: Monetary Policy (FOMC statements, minutes, projections), Enforcement Actions, Banking Applications, Regulatory Policy, Other Announcements

Notable 2011 items visible: November 2 FOMC statement and economic projections, multiple enforcement actions, Volcker Rule proposal (October 2011), capital plans rule (November 2011), coordinated central bank action on global money markets (November 30, 2011)
Published_at date is not a single date - this is a chronological archive of an entire year's press releases
why_it_matters: This listing page serves as an index to 2011 Federal Reserve documents but is not itself an archiveable research note. Individual FOMC statements, minutes, and policy documents from 2011 would need to be processed separately for proper research archiving.
macro_context: The page covers a historically significant period (2011) including the Fed's response to the post-2008 recovery, European debt crisis concerns, and implementation of Dodd-Frank provisions. However, as a listing page, it contains no specific macro analysis to extract.
notes: Non-archivable: This is a listing page containing an index of all 2011 Federal Reserve press releases. Per CORE RULE 6, listing pages are non-archiveable. To build a quality research archive, individual documents (e.g., specific FOMC statements, enforcement actions, regulatory proposals) should be processed separately with their own SOURCE METADATA. The 2011 vintage makes this historically stale relative to TODAY (2026-03-31).
